binaries.title: mrxsmb.sys - No security-relevant change (WIL feature-staging library rebuild)

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState
key_changes: PATCHED: Adds 'bts eax, 0x12' (set bit 18 / 0x40000) at 0x140040A04, forcing that bit of the recomputed cached feature-enabled-state value before it is committed with 'lock cmpxchg [r15], esi' at 0x140040A51.

PATCHED: Removes a now-redundant 'mov dword ptr [rsp+48h+arg_8], eax' store; the unpatched build wrote that slot twice (0x140040A0B and 0x140040A11), the patched build once. Net function size unchanged.

The value being reevaluated (edi) is produced by wil_details_GetCurrentFeatureEnabledState (0x140040B1C) from the OS feature-configuration store (wil_RtlStagingConfig_QueryFeatureState); it is not derived from any SMB message.
The mask 0xFFFFF63E used in the merge path preserves bit 18, so the forced bit survives subsequent masking; this is internal WIL cache bookkeeping.
changed_functions.security_notes: WIL (Windows Implementation Library) feature-staging routine, statically linked into mrxsmb.sys and many other Windows binaries. It reevaluates and atomically stores the cached enabled-state of a feature. Bit 0x40000 is an internal WIL feature-state-cache flag, not an SMB encryption-enforcement flag; neither build gates SMB encryption on it. The function is reached only from wil_details_AreDependenciesEnabled (0x140040458) and wil_details_IsEnabledFallback (0x140040C34), never from SMB NEGOTIATE / SESSION_SETUP / TREE_CONNECT processing, and its input is not attacker-controlled. This is WIL library rebuild churn, not a security fix.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
@@ wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState @ 0x1400409A8 @@
; UNPATCHED (0x140040A00):
;   0000000140040A00  mov     eax, edi
;   0000000140040A02  mov     ecx, edi
;   0000000140040A04  cmp     [rsp+48h+arg_0], 0
;   0000000140040A09  mov     esi, eax
;   0000000140040A0B  mov     dword ptr [rsp+48h+arg_8], eax
;   0000000140040A0F  jz      short loc_140040A30
;   0000000140040A11  mov     dword ptr [rsp+48h+arg_8], eax
;
; PATCHED (0x140040A00):
;   0000000140040A00  mov     eax, edi
;   0000000140040A02  mov     ecx, edi
;   0000000140040A04  bts     eax, 12h          ; ADDED: set bit 18 (0x40000)
;   0000000140040A08  cmp     [rsp+48h+arg_0], 0
;   0000000140040A0D  mov     esi, eax
;   0000000140040A0F  mov     dword ptr [rsp+48h+arg_8], eax
;
; The CAS retry loop-back at 0x140040A5C -> 0x140040A04 re-executes 'bts eax, 12h' on each retry.
; The duplicate store at former 0x140040A11 is removed.

changed_functions.name: wil_details_RegisterFeatureUsageProvider
key_changes: Adds 'and [rsp+48h+var_C], 0' at 0x1400849F0, zero-initializing a field of the on-stack structure passed to RtlRegisterFeatureUsageProvider.
Adds 'mov [rsp+48h+var_10], 1' at 0x140084A2D, initializing another field of that structure.
Registers the WIL feature-usage telemetry provider (flush callback wil_details_OnFeatureUsageProviderFlushNotification).
changed_functions.security_notes: WIL feature-usage-provider registration bookkeeping. The added stores initialize two more fields of the provider structure handed to RtlRegisterFeatureUsageProvider. Library churn; not a security fix and not attacker-reachable.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
PATCHED adds:
  00000001400849F0  and     [rsp+48h+var_C], 0
  0000000140084A2D  mov     [rsp+48h+var_10], 1
before:
  0000000140084A41  call    cs:__imp_RtlRegisterFeatureUsageProvider

summary: No security-relevant change. An independent content-matched diff of both builds shows exactly two functions with instruction-level changes, both in the statically linked WIL (Windows Implementation Library) feature-staging/feature-usage infrastructure: wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState (0x1400409A8) and wil_details_RegisterFeatureUsageProvider (0x1400849EC). The first forces an internal WIL feature-state-cache flag (bit 18 / 0x40000) on before an atomic cache write and drops a duplicate store; its input comes from the OS feature-configuration store, not the network, and it is reachable only from WIL feature-check helpers. The second initializes two additional fields of the structure passed to RtlRegisterFeatureUsageProvider. Bit 0x40000 here is an internal WIL cache flag, unrelated to SMB encryption. These are servicing changes from a WIL library rebuild, not a security fix; there is no SMB encryption-enforcement bypass and no attacker-reachable primitive.
